research.interface: coordination.k8s.io/v1 Lease read/replace with resourceVersion optimistic concurrency; researched against v1.36

official_sources.title: Lease | Kubernetes (kubernetes-api reference)
official_sources.used_for: LeaseSpec field set (holderIdentity, leaseDurationSeconds, acquireTime, renewTime, leaseTransitions), the MicroTime typing of acquireTime/renewTime, and the GET/POST/PUT operation paths under /apis/coordination.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/leases

official_sources.title: Kubernetes API Concepts
official_sources.used_for: metadata.resourceVersion optimistic-concurrency semantics for updates (409 Conflict on stale versions and re-read recovery) and the meta/v1 Status error envelope

official_sources.title: Kubernetes v1.36 official OpenAPI specification (kubernetes/kubernetes, release-1.36)
official_sources.used_for: the exact LeaseSpec property descriptions, the MicroTime definition ('version of Time with microsecond level precision', string/date-time), the PUT replace operation's 200/201/401 responses, and Status fields

research.language_rationale: The official kubernetes Python client exists, but its leader-election helper is a wrapper over exactly this read/replace-with-resourceVersion REST loop, and the harness forbids third-party packages; the Lease API is plain JSON REST, so stdlib urllib in a small scheduler sidecar is a credible, idiomatic integration surface.

verified_facts: A namespaced Lease lives at /apis/coordination.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/leases/{name}; POST to the collection creates it (201) and PUT to the name replaces it (200; 201 if the replace created it).

LeaseSpec has holderIdentity (string), leaseDurationSeconds (int32), acquireTime (MicroTime), renewTime (MicroTime) and leaseTransitions (int32); v1.36 adds optional strategy/preferredHolder for coordinated leader election, which this integration does not use.
MicroTime is 'version of Time with microsecond level precision', serialized as an RFC3339 date-time string; the canonical wire form carries six fractional digits UTC, which this seed pins.
leaseDurationSeconds is measured against the last renewTime: candidates for the lease must wait that long before force-acquiring it.

metadata.resourceVersion is the optimistic-concurrency token: a PUT whose resourceVersion does not match the current object fails with 409 Conflict ('the object has been modified'), and the documented recovery is re-reading and reapplying.
API failures are meta/v1 Status objects (kind Status, status Failure, message, reason, code); a missing lease reads as 404 with reason NotFound.
